---
title: "Spring phenology tier2 project"
author: "Constantin Zohner"
date: "Last run: `r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %B %Y')`"
subtitle: "Find optimal preseason periods for moving windows"

format:
  html:
    toc: false
    theme: default
    highlight-style: haddock
    df-print: paged
    embed-resources: true

execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
  output: true

always_allow_html: true
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

<br>

### **Driver names**

-   RH: Relative air humidity
-   SWrad: Short-wave radiation
-   Tmin: Daily minimum air temperature
-   Tmean: Daily mean air temperature
-   Tmax: Daily maximum air temperature

<br>

# 1. Setup

<details>

<summary>Packages</summary>

```{r}
require(tidyverse)
require(data.table)
require(patchwork)
require(wesanderson)
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Plot theme</summary>

```{r}
plotTheme1 = theme(legend.position   = "none",
                   legend.background = element_rect(fill=NA, linewidth=0.5, linetype="solid"),
                   legend.text       = element_text(color="black"),
                   panel.grid.major  = element_blank(),
                   panel.grid.minor  = element_blank(),
                   panel.background  = element_blank(),
                   panel.border      = element_rect(colour = "black", fill=NA),
                   axis.line         = element_line(color = "black"),
                   axis.text         = element_text(colour = "black"),
                   strip.background  = element_rect(fill=NA),
                   strip.text        = element_text(colour = 'black'),
                   plot.title        = element_text(face="bold"))
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Directories</summary>

```{r}
# Define the base directory
base.dir <- "/Users/goalie/Desktop/PhD/Publication_material/40_Spring_phenology_tier2/PEP725_Analysis"

# Build specific directories from the base
PEP.dir    <- file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_input/PEP_drivers_final_spring_MW/MW15/Merged_file")
chunkPEP.dir <- file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_input/PEP_drivers_final_spring_MW/MW15/data_chunks/0_PEP_tables")
chunk1.dir <- file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_input/PEP_drivers_final_spring_MW/MW15/data_chunks/1_Long_tables")
chunk2.dir <- file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_input/PEP_drivers_final_spring_MW/MW15/data_chunks/2_Result_tables")
chunk3.dir <- file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_input/PEP_drivers_final_spring_MW/MW15/data_chunks/3_Merged_tables")
output.dir <- file.path(base.dir, "Analysis_output/Preseason_sensitivity")
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Data</summary>

```{r}
# Load the PEP dataset from the given directory
PEP.df <- fread(paste(PEP.dir, "pep_MW15_merged.csv", sep="/")) %>% 
  
  # Drop the unwanted column 'V1'
  dplyr::select(-V1) %>% 
  
  # Clean the 'Tmax.PS100' column:
  # Some entries contain extra symbols like "$" (e.g., "7.9388$184"),
  # which prevents numeric conversion.
  # parse_number() extracts only the numeric portion (up to the first non-numeric character),
  # automatically turning invalid parts into proper numbers (e.g., "7.9388$184" → 7.9388).
  mutate(Tmax.PS100 = parse_number(Tmax.PS100))
```

</details>

<br>

# 2. Analysis

<details>

<summary>Preseason correlations</summary>

```{r}
# define phenology classes
phen_classes <- unique(PEP.df$phenology)


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Save PEP data in chunks
#-------------------------------------------------------

# empty list
pep_chunks <- list()

# loop
for (ph in phen_classes) {
  message("Processing: ", ph)
  
  #filter phenology chunk
  df_chunk <- PEP.df %>%
    filter(phenology == ph)
  
  # save file as RDS
  saveRDS(df_chunk, file = file.path(chunkPEP.dir, paste0("pep_MW15_", ph, ".rds")))
  rm(df_chunk)
  gc()
}


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Reshape table to long format
#-------------------------------------------------------

# empty list
all_preseason <- list()

# loop
for (ph in phen_classes) {
  message("Processing: ", ph)
  
  df_chunk <- PEP.df %>%
    
    #filter phenology chunk
    filter(phenology == ph) %>%
    
    #select columns
    dplyr::select(phenology, timeseries, year, MWyear, species, s_id, leaf_out,
                  starts_with(c("Tmean.PS", "Tmin.PS", "Tmax.PS", "SWrad.PS", "RH.PS"))) %>%
    #long format
    pivot_longer(cols = starts_with(c("Tm","SWrad","RH")),
                 names_to = "preseason", values_to = "value") %>%
    #create preseason length and variable class columns
    mutate(preseason_length = readr::parse_number(gsub("[.]", "", preseason)), #keep only numbers in string
           var_class = gsub("\\..*","", preseason)) %>%
    dplyr::select(-preseason)
  
  # save file as RDS
  saveRDS(df_chunk, file = file.path(chunk1.dir, paste0("preseason_", ph, ".rds")))
  rm(df_chunk)
  gc()
}
rm(PEP.df)


#-------------------------------------------------------
# Correlation coefficients
#-------------------------------------------------------

# load data chunks (chunked per phenology and saved as RDS)
chunk.files <- list.files(chunk1.dir, pattern="\\.rds$", full.names=TRUE)

# empty list
results_list <- list()

# loop
for (file in chunk.files) {
  message("Processing correlations for: ", tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file)))
  
  df_chunk <- readRDS(file)
  
  res <- df_chunk %>%
    group_by(phenology, timeseries, species, s_id, MWyear, var_class, preseason_length) %>%
    summarise(coeff = cor(leaf_out, value, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
              .groups = "drop")
  
  # Save each chunk's result separately to avoid holding it all in RAM
  saveRDS(res, file = file.path(chunk2.dir, paste0("resultsLM_", 
                                                   tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(file)), 
                                                   ".rds")))
  rm(df_chunk, res)
  gc()
}

# Combine all correlation results later:
resultsLM <- lapply(list.files(chunk2.dir, pattern="resultsLM_.*\\.rds$", full.names=TRUE), readRDS)
resultsLM <- data.table::rbindlist(resultsLM)

#keep only models with highest absolute correlation coefficients
resultsLM2 = resultsLM %>%   
  group_by(phenology,timeseries,MWyear,var_class) %>% 
  top_n(1, abs(coeff)) %>%
  ungroup()

#Mean preseason length
resultsLM2 %>% 
  group_by(phenology, species, var_class) %>% 
  summarize(meanPre = mean(preseason_length)) %>% 
  ungroup()
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Plot preseason correlations</summary>

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------------
# Mean across all timeseries and species
#-------------------------------------------------------

plot.estimate = resultsLM %>%
  mutate(var_class = factor(var_class, levels = c("RH","SWrad","Tmin","Tmean","Tmax")),
         phenology = factor(phenology, levels = c("leaf_out","leaf_out_GDD.chill.DL","leaf_out_GDD.chill.hi","leaf_out_GDD.chill.lo","leaf_out_GDD"))) %>% 
  ggplot()+
  aes(x=preseason_length, y=coeff, 
      colour=MWyear, group=MWyear) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0)+
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom="line", size = .7) +
  scale_color_gradientn(colours = rainbow(5))+
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom="errorbar", linewidth = 0.5, width=0) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", geom="point", size = 1) +
  xlab("Preseason length (days)") +
  ylab("Correlation coefficient") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.85, 0.65), xlim=c(14,116))+
  plotTheme1+
  theme(legend.position   = "top",
        legend.title      = element_blank(),
        legend.key        = element_blank(),
        legend.text.align = 0,
        #strip.text.x      = element_blank()
        ) +
  facet_grid(var_class~phenology)
rm(resultsLM)

plot.estimate

#-------------------------------------------------------
# Plot best preseason lengths
#-------------------------------------------------------

ModelLabs <- c("RH","SWR",expression("T"["min"]), expression("T"["mean"]),expression("T"["max"]))

plot.length = resultsLM2 %>%
  mutate(var_class = factor(var_class, levels = c("RH","SWrad","Tmin","Tmean","Tmax")),
         phenology = factor(phenology, levels = c("leaf_out","leaf_out_GDD.chill.DL","leaf_out_GDD.chill.hi","leaf_out_GDD.chill.lo","leaf_out_GDD"))) %>% 
  dplyr::select(phenology,species,var_class,preseason_length)%>%
  ggplot()+
  aes(x=phenology, y=preseason_length, colour=var_class, fill=var_class, alpha=0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.colour = NA)+
  scale_color_manual(values = wes_palette("Zissou1", n = 5)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = wes_palette("Zissou1", n = 5)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels= ModelLabs)+
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(10, 117))+
  xlab("") +
  ylab("Best preseason length (days)") +
  plotTheme1+
  facet_wrap(~var_class, ncol=1)

plot.length

#-------------------------------------------------------
# Arrange and save plots #
#-------------------------------------------------------

pdf(paste(output.dir,"Preseason_estimates_MW15.pdf",sep="/"), width=6, height=8, useDingbats=FALSE)
plot.estimate
dev.off()
rm(plot.estimate)

pdf(paste(output.dir,"Preseason_length_MW15.pdf",sep="/"), width=4, height=7, useDingbats=FALSE)
plot.length
dev.off()
rm(plot.length)
```

</details>

<details>

<summary>Add preseason data to PEP data</summary>

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------------
# Merge best preseason climate variables into PEP data
# (chunked by phenology group to avoid memory overload)
#-------------------------------------------------------

for (ph in phen_classes) {

  # Build expected file names
  file1 <- paste0("preseason_", ph, ".rds") # preseason chunk
  file2 <- paste0("pep_MW15_", ph, ".rds")  # PEP chunk
  
  # Build full paths
  path1 <- file.path(chunk1.dir, file1)
  path2 <- file.path(chunkPEP.dir, file2)
  
  # Check both files exist before proceeding
  if (!file.exists(path1) | !file.exists(path2)) {
    warning(paste("Skipping phenology:", ph, " (missing file)"))
    next
  }

  message("Processing phenology: ", ph)

  # Load datasets
  preseason.df <- readRDS(path1) %>% 
    dplyr::select(-c(s_id, leaf_out, species))
  PEP.df <- readRDS(path2)
  results.df <- resultsLM2 %>% filter(phenology == ph)
  
  #-------------------------------------------------------
  # 1. Add best preseason *climate variable values*
  #-------------------------------------------------------
  
  # Keep only rows that match the best models (from resultsLM2),
  # then pivot to a wide format so each variable is its own column.
  preseason.filtered <- preseason.df %>%
    semi_join(results.df, by = c("phenology", "timeseries", "MWyear", "var_class", "preseason_length")) %>%
    dplyr::select(phenology, timeseries, MWyear, year, var_class, value) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = var_class, values_from = value)
  
  # Join these climate variables into the main PEP data (filtering to this phenology group)
  PEP.joined <- PEP.df %>% 
    filter(phenology == ph) %>% 
    inner_join(preseason.filtered, by = c("phenology", "MWyear", "year", "timeseries"))
  rm(preseason.filtered, PEP.df)
  gc()
  
  
  #-------------------------------------------------------
  # 2. Add preseason *lengths* (number of days per variable)
  #-------------------------------------------------------
  
  preseason.length.filtered <- preseason.df %>%
    filter(phenology == ph) %>%
    semi_join(results.df, by=c('phenology','timeseries','MWyear','var_class','preseason_length')) %>%
    dplyr::select(phenology, timeseries, MWyear, year, var_class, preseason_length) %>%
    mutate(var_class = paste(var_class, "length", sep="_")) %>% 
    pivot_wider(names_from = var_class, values_from = preseason_length)
  
  # Join the lengths into the combined PEP data
  PEP.joined <- PEP.joined %>%
    inner_join(preseason.length.filtered, by = c("phenology", "MWyear", "year", "timeseries"))
  rm(preseason.length.filtered, preseason.df)
  
  
  #-------------------------------------------------------
  # 3. For each of Tmean and Tmax, extract the correct SD and slope values
  #    based on the chosen preseason length (10, 20, ..., 120 days)
  #-------------------------------------------------------
  
  for (var in c("Tmean", "Tmax")) {
    len_col <- paste0(var, "_length")  # The column holding the chosen length
    
    # Identify all available intra-annual SD and slope columns (e.g., Tmean.IntraSD.PS10)
    sd_cols <- grep(paste0("^", var, "\\.IntraSD\\.PS"), names(PEP.joined), value=TRUE)
    sd_lookup <- setNames(sd_cols, sub(paste0(var, "\\.IntraSD\\.PS"), "", sd_cols))  # map length -> colname
    
    slope_cols <- grep(paste0("^", var, "\\.slope\\.PS"), names(PEP.joined), value=TRUE)
    slope_lookup <- setNames(slope_cols, sub(paste0(var, "\\.slope\\.PS"), "", slope_cols))
    
    # For each row, find which column corresponds to its chosen preseason length
    sd_targets <- sd_lookup[as.character(PEP.joined[[len_col]])]
    slope_targets <- slope_lookup[as.character(PEP.joined[[len_col]])]
    
    # Extract values row-wise (this is the slowest step; see note below for faster options)
    PEP.joined[[paste0(var, ".IntraSD")]] <- mapply(function(row, col) {
      if (!is.na(col)) PEP.joined[[col]][row] else NA_real_
    }, seq_len(nrow(PEP.joined)), sd_targets)
    
    PEP.joined[[paste0(var, ".slope")]] <- mapply(function(row, col) {
      if (!is.na(col)) PEP.joined[[col]][row] else NA_real_
    }, seq_len(nrow(PEP.joined)), slope_targets)
  }
  
  
  #-------------------------------------------------------
  # 4. Compute start dates of climate-sensitive periods and clean up
  #-------------------------------------------------------
  
  PEP.joined <- PEP.joined %>%
    mutate(
      Tmean_start = leaf_out_mean - Tmean_length,
      Tmin_start  = leaf_out_mean - Tmin_length,
      Tmax_start  = leaf_out_mean - Tmax_length,
      SWrad_start = leaf_out_mean - SWrad_length,
      RH_start    = leaf_out_mean - RH_length
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(-contains(".PS"))  # Drop original per-PS columns (no longer needed)
  
  # Save the final combined dataset for this phenology group
  saveRDS(PEP.joined, file = file.path(chunk3.dir, paste0("final_", ph, ".rds")))
  
  # Free memory aggressively before next loop
  rm(PEP.joined)
  gc()
}


#-------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Combine all processed phenology groups into one dataset
#-------------------------------------------------------

# List all the final per-phenology files
final_chunks <- list.files(chunk3.dir, pattern = "^final_.*\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE)

# Load and combine them efficiently
final_PEP <- rbindlist(lapply(final_chunks, readRDS))

# Save the combined dataset 
fwrite(final_PEP, file = file.path(PEP.dir, "pep_MW15_merged_final.csv"))
```

</details>

<br>

# 3. Reproducibility

<details>

<summary>Show</summary>

```{r}
## datetime
Sys.time()

## session info
sessionInfo()
```

</details>
